facts.statement: Progressive is classified under SIC code 'Fire, Marine & Casualty Insurance' and trades on the NYSE under the ticker PGR.
Progressive reported total revenue of $62,109,000,000 for FY2025.
Progressive reported net income of $3,903,000,000 for FY2025.
Progressive generated operating cash flow of $10,643,000,000 for FY2025.
Progressive reported stockholders' equity of $20,277,000,000 as of FY2025 year-end.
Progressive reports three operating segments: Personal Lines, Commercial Lines, and Other Indemnity, with data reported for fiscal years 2023, 2024, and 2025.
For FY2025, net premiums written were $72,558 million in Personal Lines, $10,613 million in Commercial Lines, and $3 million in Other Indemnity, for a total of $83,174 million.
Total net premiums written grew from $61,550 million in FY2023 to $74,424 million in FY2024 and $83,174 million in FY2025.

As of December 31, 2025, Progressive's total investments were $97,373 million at fair value, consisting of $82,866 million in fixed maturities, $4,502 million in equity securities, and $10,005 million in short-term investments.

U.S. Government and government agency bonds constituted the largest fixed maturity holding at $43,298 million fair value as of December 31, 2025, followed by corporate and other debt securities at $18,132 million and asset-backed securities at $16,257 million.
For FY2025, Progressive ceded $1,186 million in premiums to other companies and assumed $0, resulting in net premiums earned of $81,661 million; the percentage of assumed premiums to net was 0%.
In FY2025, Progressive paid $2,871 million in dividends to common shareholders, compared to $674 million in FY2024 and $234 million in FY2023.
Progressive acquired $74 million of treasury shares in the open market in FY2025, compared to $13 million in FY2024 and $46 million in FY2023.
Progressive's parent company had $6,897 million in long-term debt as of December 31, 2025, essentially unchanged from $6,893 million at December 31, 2024.

Progressive has multiple senior note issuances outstanding, including 6.625% Senior Notes due 2029 ($300M), 6.25% Senior Notes due 2032 ($400M), 4.35% Senior Notes due 2044 ($350M), 4.125% Senior Notes due 2047 ($850M), and 4.20% Senior Notes due 2048 ($600M), among others.
Susan Patricia Griffith serves as President and Chief Executive Officer of The Progressive Corporation, and John P. Sauerland serves as Vice President and Chief Financial Officer.
Carl G. Joyce serves as Vice President and Chief Accounting Officer of The Progressive Corporation.
Lawton W. Fitt serves as Chairperson of the Board, and the Board nominated 11 directors for election at the May 8, 2026 Annual Meeting, each to serve a one-year term.
As of the March 13, 2026 record date, Progressive had 584,962,821 common shares outstanding.
In 2025, Progressive achieved companywide policies in force growth of 10% and net premiums written growth of 12%, with a debt-to-capital ratio of 18.5% at year-end.

Over the five-year period ending 2025, Progressive delivered annualized rates of return on net income and comprehensive income of 26.0% and 25.2% respectively, outpaced the S&P 500 by 1.6x for total shareholder return, and achieved annualized net premiums written growth of 15%.

Progressive's five-year annualized underwriting margin of 8.3% and net premiums written growth of 15% exceeded the insurance industry's underwriting margin of (0.4)% and net premiums written growth of 7%, based on 2024 U.S. private passenger auto premiums written.
Progressive maintained underwriting profitability aligned with its long-term 96 combined ratio target over the five-year period ending 2025.
Progressive ranked in the 99th percentile of companies in its annual engagement and culture survey in 2025 and has approximately 70,000 employees.
Progressive has a Dodd-Frank Clawback Policy and an Insider Trading Policy governing securities transactions by directors, officers, and insiders, both filed as exhibits to its annual reports.
PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP (PCAOB ID: 238) serves as Progressive's independent registered public accounting firm, with the Board recommending ratification of their appointment for 2026.
The Progressive Corporation parent company received $10,151 million in dividends from subsidiaries in FY2025, up from $3,667 million in FY2024 and $399 million in FY2023.

At December 31, 2025, $13.0 billion of marketable securities were available in a non-insurance investment subsidiary for the parent company to meet holding company obligations, up from $6.2 billion at year-end 2024.
